items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This livestream provides a practical deep dive into Amazon EBS cost optimization, showing how to identify and right-size overprovisioned volumes, leverage newer EBS volume types, and eliminate waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes. It includes real-world examples, monitoring tools, and actionable strategies to cut EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right-size overprovisioned EBS volumes
Leverage latest EBS volume types to match performance with cost
Eliminate waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes
Demonstrate monitoring tools for storage utilization
Share real-world examples and actionable cost-saving strategies
takeaways: Right-sizing and adopting appropriate EBS volume types can lower storage costs while preserving performance
Regularly auditing and removing unused snapshots/old volumes prevents ongoing budget drains
Monitoring storage utilization enables targeted optimization and better allocation of resources
Actionable, example-driven strategies allow teams to realize measurable EBS savings quickly

items.title: Let's talk about valuations ft. Tim Deal

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Valuation is being enhanced with technology and AI, and PwC supports clients to improve efficiency, quality, and consistency throughout the valuation process. The video highlights a tech- and AI-driven "glow-up" for valuations and PwC's role in that effort.
bullets: Valuation work is receiving a tech and AI upgrade.
PwC helps clients boost efficiency, quality, and consistency in valuations.
Support is positioned as covering every step of the valuation process.
Themes include artificial intelligence, innovation, and business technology.
takeaways: AI and tech-enabled valuation tools aim to improve efficiency—useful when assessing grid or data center investments.
Higher consistency and quality in valuations can strengthen comparative analyses for site selection and asset valuation.
Relying on updated, technology-backed valuation processes may help streamline due diligence for grid/data center decisions.
Engaging advisors who apply AI and tech to valuations could increase confidence in valuation outputs used for planning and investment.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre parcel in upstate New York (reported as $27K of land) intending to build Airbnb properties and spent three years commuting from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin by himself, with more cabins added afterward. As of September 2025 his property—which includes luxury cabins renting around $700/night—has brought in over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015 purchase of a five-acre property (reported $27K) in upstate New York.
Chris Broomfield built an initial A-frame cabin over three years with weekend commutes from Connecticut.
Multiple additional cabins were developed on the site after the first build.
Cabins marketed as luxury rentals at about $700 per night.
By September 2025 the property had generated over $2.1 million in revenue.
takeaways: High per-unit revenue potential from luxury short-term rentals suggests concentrated economic value at rural hospitality sites.
Development of multiple rental units on a single rural parcel can create sustained demand for reliable local infrastructure and services.
Remote, owner-led construction projects may scale into significant revenue-generating assets, highlighting the need to anticipate growth when planning capacity and connectivity.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this KPMG ON AIR podcast with Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski explains how to view operational changes as restructurings from a transfer pricing and valuation perspective. It covers recognizing significant changes (including EBIT analysis), reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), the role of valuations, common pitfalls, practical examples like moving production/distribution or transferring employees and contracts, and ends with a checklist of actions.
bullets: Defines restructuring in transfer pricing context and how to spot significant changes.
Explains EBIT analysis and criteria for assessing materiality.
Stresses importance of timely documentation and its long-term benefits.
Highlights reporting obligations (TPR, MDR) and valuation roles with common errors.
Gives practical examples (relocating production/distribution, employee/contract transfers) and a checklist for firms.
takeaways: If you relocate or reassign data center functions, treat the change as a potential transfer-pricing restructuring and analyze EBIT impact.
Prepare transfer-pricing documentation early to reduce future compliance risk and administrative burden.
Assess whether changes trigger reporting obligations (TPR, MDR) across jurisdictions before implementing moves.
Include valuations in planning to avoid common pricing and allocation mistakes when shifting assets, people or contracts.
